title: Go Messaging Observability, Capacity, and Alarms · Mastery Check
summary: A release board compares near-miss Go and RabbitMQ decisions for go messaging observability, capacity, and alarms and accepts only bounded ownership, causal evidence, and an explicit live-broker transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a low-cardinality messaging telemetry and capacity plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Instrument publish, confirm, return, route, deliver, process, ack, retry, dead-letter, and effect phases with bounded identities and causal links; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Interpret ready, unacknowledged, total, ingress, delivery, ack, redelivery, consumers, consumer capacity, connections, channels, and node resource metrics together; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Model arrival rate, service rate, concurrency, oldest age, burst, retry amplification, byte volume, and recovery time for capacity decisions; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Message Flow Telemetry · changed case · Quiz Message Flow Telemetry
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 8573 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine backlog, age, alarm, consumer, and bounded-capacity evidence; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then fail the canary and prove older Go consumers remain compatible. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Instrument publish, confirm, return, route, deliver, process, ack, retry, dead-letter, and effect phases with bounded identities and causal links; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
